summary: Agent landscape active: Anthropic detailed Claude Code's origin and new Sonnet 5; Google released ADK Go 2.0; Microsoft launched Flint (visualization language for agents) and teased a Copilot OS. Open-source saw secure coding agent (SecureVector), agent execution tracing (agent-inspect), and lightweight infrastructure (Kuma, Sandbox alternatives). Security concerns remain central: CVE in Cline, MCP blast radius discussions, and need for provenance in agent task completion.

candidates.title: Claude Code: from internal CLI to Anthropic’s coding agent (official inside story)
candidates.why_it_matters: Primary source on Claude Code design decisions and containment lessons.

candidates.title: Google announces ADK Go 2.0: graph-based workflow engine, human-in-the-loop
candidates.why_it_matters: Major platform update for building reliable multi-agent apps.

candidates.title: Microsoft open-sources Flint: visualization language for AI agents
candidates.why_it_matters: New orthogonal vector for agent workflow comprehension.

candidates.title: User builds Claude Code loop that forces agents to prove 'done' with evidence
candidates.why_it_matters: Direct operator fix for agent task-completion verification gap.; social/discussion source (keep; label evidence)

candidates.title: Bluesky: giving AI agents Azure creds ≠ junior dev SSH (no tiredness, no questioning)
candidates.why_it_matters: Operator safety concern about excessive trust in agent execution.; social/discussion source (keep; label evidence)

candidates.title: CVE-2026-59723: High (8.8) vulnerability in Cline coding agent prior to 3.0.30
candidates.why_it_matters: Critical security advisory for popular autonomous coding agent.

candidates.title: rivet-dev/agentos: lighter, cheaper sandbox alternative with agent orchestration
candidates.why_it_matters: Sandbox infra cost and speed improvement for agent execution.

candidates.title: mcp-permission-guard: tool call allow/deny rules for AI agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Practical access control for MCP tool ecosystems.

candidates.title: agent-inspect: local execution trees for TypeScript AI agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Terminal-based agent run inspection (tool calls, LLM calls, failures).

candidates.title: SecureVector: audit every tool call, block prompt injection/data leaks
candidates.why_it_matters: Security observability for agent tool calls across multiple agents.

candidates.title: MCP Doctor: debug MCP server boot & JSON-RPC handshake failures
candidates.why_it_matters: Concrete diagnostic tool for MCP setup issues (user workflow gap).

candidates.title: GitHub Copilot in VS Code: June 2026 release changelog
candidates.why_it_matters: Official product update for widely-used AI coding assistant.

gaps: Missing user_workflow: dark horse field report from HN/Reddit/Bluesky on multi-agent coordination in production
Missing mainstream_product: OpenAI Codex 0.144.0-alpha.4 changelog details not included (prerelease only)
